description: CLI tool that queries Kubernetes health endpoints across namespaces, aggregates results, and outputs a summary. Built with Go 1.23, Cobra CLI, client-go, slog, testify, and golang.org/x/sync/errgroup. Supports kubeconfig context switching, concurrent probing, JSON and table output formats. Exit code 0 when all healthy, 1 when any unhealthy.

quantitative: Final container image stays under 20 MiB
prohibitions: MUST NOT add a web UI, database, or metrics endpoint
MUST NOT modify or weaken existing test assertions to make a story pass
MUST NOT add runtime dependencies outside Cobra, client-go, slog, testify, and golang.org/x/sync without approval
requirements: MUST support kubeconfig path and context selection
MUST support JSON and table output
MUST return exit code 1 when any pod is unhealthy

userStories.title: Initialize Go module and root CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean Go project with the root Cobra CLI so that later stories can add behavior incrementally.
acceptanceCriteria: go.mod initialized with module github.com/user/kube-healthcheck
cmd/root.go sets up Cobra root command with --kubeconfig and --context persistent flags
main.go calls cmd.Execute()
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not add runtime behavior beyond root CLI setup
Does not introduce dependencies outside Cobra, slog, testify, and golangci-lint tooling

userStories.title: Add check subcommand and toolchain
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the check subcommand and quality tooling scaffolded so that later stories can add real behavior safely.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/check.go defines a 'check' subcommand and wires it into the root command
Running ./kube-healthcheck check --help prints usage
Makefile has targets: build, test, lint, clean
.golangci-lint.yaml exists with errcheck, govet, staticcheck enabled
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not implement discovery, probing, or reporting behavior yet
Does not introduce dependencies outside Cobra, slog, testify, and golangci-lint tooling

userStories.title: Kubernetes client initialization from kubeconfig
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool to connect to my cluster using my kubeconfig so that I don't need separate auth config.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/kube/client.go exports NewClient(kubeconfigPath, context string) (*kubernetes.Clientset, error)
Respects --kubeconfig flag first, then KUBECONFIG env var, then ~/.kube/config
Respects --context flag to override kubeconfig's current-context
Returns descriptive error when kubeconfig file doesn't exist
Returns descriptive error when specified context doesn't exist in kubeconfig
Unit test with table-driven cases covers: explicit path, env var fallback, default path, missing file, bad context
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not hardcode kubeconfig paths or cluster credentials in source
Does not bypass the --context flag when it is provided

userStories.title: Pod discovery across namespaces
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool to find pods with health probes so that I get a cluster-wide health view.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/kube/discovery.go exports DiscoverPods(ctx, clientset, namespaces []string, allNamespaces bool) ([]PodInfo, error)
PodInfo struct includes: Name, Namespace, HealthEndpoint (path+port from readiness/liveness probe), ProbeType (readiness|liveness)
When allNamespaces=true, lists pods across all namespaces
When allNamespaces=false and namespaces=[ns1,ns2], lists pods only in those namespaces
When allNamespaces=false and namespaces is empty, uses the caller-provided default namespace
Only returns pods that have HTTP GET readiness or liveness probes defined
Unit test uses fake kubernetes.Clientset with test fixtures
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not return pods without HTTP GET readiness or liveness probes
Does not ignore namespace filters when --namespace or --all-namespaces is set

userStories.title: Concurrent health probing with timeout
userStories.description: As a user, I want health checks to run in parallel so that the tool is fast even with many pods.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/kube/probe.go exports ProbePods(ctx, pods []PodInfo, concurrency int, timeout time.Duration) ([]ProbeResult, error)
ProbeResult struct includes: PodInfo, StatusCode int, Latency time.Duration, Error error, Healthy bool
Healthy is true when StatusCode is 200, false otherwise
Uses errgroup with semaphore pattern for concurrency limiting
concurrency argument controls max parallel probes
timeout argument controls per-probe timeout
Probes that timeout return Error with context.DeadlineExceeded and Healthy=false
go test -race ./internal/kube/ passes (no race conditions)
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not spawn more concurrent probes than the configured --concurrency value
Does not report timed-out probes as healthy
userStories.notes: Use port-forwarding or in-cluster direct pod IP access for probing. The probe approach should be noted in progress.txt for future iterations.

userStories.title: Aggregate results and format output
userStories.description: As a user, I want health check results formatted independently of the probing implementation so that output behavior can be built and tested in parallel.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/report/report.go exports FormatResults(rows []ResultRow, format string) (string, error)
ResultRow struct includes: Namespace, Pod, Endpoint, Status, Latency, Healthy
Summary struct includes: TotalPods, HealthyCount, UnhealthyCount, PerNamespace map[string]NamespaceSummary
format="json" returns valid JSON (parseable by jq)
format="table" returns an aligned table with columns: NAMESPACE | POD | ENDPOINT | STATUS | LATENCY
Table output lists unhealthy pods first, then healthy pods
Unit tests cover both JSON and table output with sample data including healthy and unhealthy pods
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not emit invalid JSON when format="json" is selected
Does not reorder table output to hide unhealthy pods below healthy ones

userStories.title: Wire everything in the check command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run a single command that discovers, probes, and reports so that the tool is usable end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: cmd/check.go wires together: client init → discovery → probing → formatting → output
All flags registered: --kubeconfig, --context, --namespace, --all-namespaces, --concurrency, --timeout, --output
--output=json writes valid JSON to stdout
--output=table writes an aligned table to stdout
Exit code 1 when any pod is unhealthy, exit code 0 when all healthy
Running ./kube-healthcheck check --help shows all flags with descriptions
Errors at any stage produce descriptive message to stderr and exit code 1
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not bypass client init, discovery, probing, or reporting stages
Does not print operational errors to stdout

userStories.title: Dockerfile and README
userStories.description: As a DevOps engineer, I want a container image and usage docs so that the tool can run in CI pipelines.
acceptanceCriteria: Dockerfile uses multi-stage build: golang:1.23-alpine builder, alpine:3.20 runtime
docker build -t kube-healthcheck . exits 0
docker run --rm kube-healthcheck --help exits 0 and prints usage
README.md documents: installation (go install + docker), all flags, output formats, exit codes
README.md includes example GitHub Actions CI snippet
README.md includes example usage commands for common scenarios
go build ./... exits 0
go test ./... -v -count=1 passes with no failures
golangci-lint run exits 0
antiCriteria: Does not use a single-stage Docker build that leaves Go toolchain artifacts in the runtime image
Does not document flags or exit codes that the CLI does not actually support
quantitativeCriteria: `docker image inspect kube-healthcheck --format '{{.Size}}'` returns a value < 20971520
